description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a dual-wing configuration, painted in olive green with roundel insignias on the upper wings, reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The fuselage is streamlined and metallic, with a wooden propeller mounted at the nose and a visible radial engine. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, likely designed for a single pilot. The tail section features a conventional horizontal stabilizer and a single vertical fin, both painted in a lighter color. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The overall design suggests a lightweight, maneuverable aircraft suitable for reconnaissance or training roles in an urban park or museum setting.
description: A vintage olive green biplane with dual wings, open cockpit, wooden propeller, and fixed landing gear, featuring roundel insignias and a classic tail design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual wings, wooden propeller, radial engine, and roundel insignias on the upper wings.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the stacked biplane wings, open cockpit, and the length of the fuselage.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section with horizontal stabilizer, single vertical fin, and the fixed landing gear.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view emphasizing the wing structure, cockpit placement, and tail assembly.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing.
